headline: SME Digitalisation Grant Reaches Only 12% of Eligible Businesses
context: MDEC's SME Digitalisation Programme disbursed grants to only 48,000 of the 400,000 eligible micro and small enterprises by end-2025, with application complexity and digital literacy barriers preventing uptake among the businesses that need it most.

cards.big: Only 12% uptake — 352,000 SMEs missed digitalisation grants because the application process itself requires digital skills they lack
cards.sub: RM5,000 grant per SME requires 14-page online application with business registration, tax clearance, and bank verification

cards.big: Digitised SMEs show 22% higher revenue growth than non-digital peers — the grant's RM5,000 generates RM18,000 in average returns
cards.sub: Total programme budget of RM1.2 billion reached only RM240 million in disbursements — 80% unspent

cards.big: Malay-medium SME owners in rural areas show 78% drop-off rate during English-language online application process
cards.sub: Grant design assumes digital baseline that 68% of target SMEs don't possess — programme helps the already-capable

cards.big: MDEC outsourced programme delivery to 8 technology providers who receive commissions regardless of SME adoption success
cards.sub: No outcome tracking beyond grant disbursement — whether SMEs actually use the technology purchased is unmonitored

cards.big: A digitalisation programme that only reaches digitally literate businesses is like offering swimming lessons only to people who can already swim
Should SME digitalisation grants use a walk-in application process at TEKUN offices with Malay-language support and hands-on onboarding?
